seoTitle: How to Create a Macro in Zendesk for Faster Replies
metaDescription: Learn how to create a macro in Zendesk that bundles a canned response with automatic field updates like status, priority, and tags in one click.
intro: Typing the same reply and manually updating ticket fields every time slows down agents handling repetitive issues. Zendesk macros bundle a canned text response together with automatic field changes into a single click. This guide covers building a macro from scratch and testing it on a live ticket.

steps.heading: Open the macros manager
steps.body: In Admin Center, expand the Workspaces menu and click Agent Tools, then Macros.

steps.heading: Start a new macro
steps.body: Click Create Macro to launch the primary settings page.

steps.heading: Name it and set availability
steps.body: Input a search-optimized title and assign availability permissions, either for all agents or just yourself.

steps.heading: Write the response text
steps.body: Go to Actions, choose Comment/Description, and write your standard response text.

steps.heading: Stack additional field actions
steps.body: Click Add Action to stack ticket field changes, like setting status to Pending or adjusting priority.

steps.heading: Save and test
steps.body: Review your complete sequence and click Create, then open an active ticket to verify the macro loads correctly from the toolbar.

faq.question: Can a macro do more than just insert text?
faq.answer: Yes, macros can simultaneously update ticket status, priority, and tags alongside the canned text response.

faq.question: Can I limit a macro to just my own use?
faq.answer: Yes, availability permissions let you scope a macro to personal use or share it account-wide.

faq.question: Is there a way to organize a large number of macros?
faq.answer: Using a naming convention with a folder-style separator like :: in the title helps classify macros into groups.

faq.question: How do I trigger a macro while replying?
faq.answer: Macros can be applied from the bottom toolbar or through inline keyboard slash command shortcuts.
